expectations.evidence: 02-websocket-server.md explicitly states: 'WebSockets are chosen over SSE because chat requires full-duplex communication — both sending and receiving messages — and SSE is one-directional.' This documents the WebSocket vs SSE decision with clear reasoning in the todo context.

The 8 todos decompose the feature by distinct concern: 01=database schema (data model), 02=WebSocket server (real-time infra), 03=REST API routes (API), 04=WebSocket client hook (client transport), 05=state management (client store), 06=UI components (rendering), 07=read receipts (cross-cutting feature), 08=notifications and polish. No todo is split by arbitrary size; each maps to a coherent technical concern.

Read receipts (07) is kept as a single todo covering the full end-to-end flow: REST mark-read call, WebSocket server handler, Zustand store update, MessageBubble UI rendering, group/DM display variants, and offline edge case — all tightly coupled items in one todo rather than split across multiple. Similarly, 02-websocket-server.md covers the full server setup including auth, message handlers, and heartbeat in one cohesive todo.

All checklist items describe specific implementable actions with file paths, method signatures, and behavior details. Examples: 'Create app/api/chat/rooms/route.ts: GET returns the authenticated user's room list with last message preview and unread count; POST creates a new room (accepts type, name, memberIds[])' (03); 'Implement exponential backoff reconnection: on close or error, wait min(1000 * 2^attempt, 30000) ms before reconnecting; reset attempt counter on successful open' (04). None are vague directives.

Client-side state management is entirely in 05-chat-state-management.md (Zustand store, types, actions, hooks/use-chat-actions.ts wiring WebSocket events to the store) while UI component rendering is entirely in 06-chat-ui-components.md (ChatSidebar, MessageList, MessageBubble, MessageInput, etc.). The WebSocket client hook itself is a separate todo (04). These are three distinct, dedicated todos.

08-notifications-and-polish.md includes: 'Write integration tests for the WebSocket send_message and mark_read flows using vitest and a test WebSocket client.' This specifies the test framework (vitest), the specific test scenarios (send_message and mark_read flows), and the tooling (test WebSocket client). However, no explicit test file paths (e.g., __tests__/chat.test.ts) are provided.

Multiple todos state architectural assumptions in context paragraphs. 02-websocket-server.md: 'A custom Next.js server (server.ts) is needed to attach the WebSocket server to the same HTTP port.' 03-chat-api-routes.md: 'These REST endpoints handle operations that do not need to be real-time' and 'POST is a fallback HTTP message send for clients without WebSocket support.' 07-read-receipts.md implies a read-tracking strategy using both lastReadAt and per-message MessageReadReceipt. 04-websocket-client-hook.md describes the reconnection policy: exponential backoff with min(1000 * 2^attempt, 30000) ms.

04-websocket-client-hook.md defines: 'Define a ChatEvent discriminated union type covering all server-sent event shapes: message_received, read_update, member_joined, member_left, connected, error.' 02-websocket-server.md defines: 'Define a SocketClient type that extends ws.WebSocket with fields: userId: string, rooms: Set<string>.' 05-chat-state-management.md defines: 'Define types: Room, Message, Member, ReadReceipt mirroring the Prisma models but as plain client-side objects.' 03-chat-api-routes.md mentions Zod: 'Add input validation with zod for all POST/PATCH request bodies.'
